title: Wire in-process MCP server into Tauri AppState
analysis:
This task connects the existing reify-mcp crate to the Tauri GUI backend by implementing the ReifyToolContext trait for the engine state accessible through AppState. The architecture follows the existing pattern: EngineSession is behind Arc<Mutex<>>, and trait implementations delegate to EngineSession methods.

Key observations from codebase exploration:
- ReifyToolContext trait has 15 methods (8 read, 5 write, 2 navigation) defined in crates/reify-mcp/src/context.rs
- AppState holds engine: Arc<Mutex<EngineSession>>, last_state: Mutex<Option<GuiState>>, watcher: Mutex<Option<FileWatcher>>
- EngineSession has methods: load_from_source, load_file, update_source, set_parameter, export, get_source_location, build_gui_state
- EngineSession.source_map is HashMap<String, String> (normalized key like 'module.ri' -> content)
- Existing commands use _impl pattern for testability (e.g., set_parameter_impl takes &Mutex<EngineSession>)
- MCP types (ParameterInfo, ConstraintInfo, etc.) closely mirror GUI types (ValueData, ConstraintData) but are decoupled
- ToolRegistry.call_tool dispatches by name to handler closures taking (Value, &dyn ReifyToolContext)
- register_all_tools registers all 16 tools; McpServer wraps registry+context
- Tests use reify-test-support crate with bracket_source() and MockGeometryKernel

Approach: Create TauriToolContext struct in new mcp_context.rs module. It holds Arc<Mutex<EngineSession>> and an optional event emitter callback for navigation tools. Each ReifyToolContext method locks the engine, delegates to EngineSession, and converts between GUI types and MCP types. A mcp_tool_call_impl function creates a ToolRegistry and dispatches tool calls. The Tauri command wrapper in main.rs constructs the context with an AppHandle-based event emitter.

Review fix (stale_state_cache): The mcp_tool_call Tauri command must update state.last_state and emit delta events after tool calls, matching the pattern used by set_parameter, update_source, and open_file_engine. Without this, MCP-triggered mutations leave last_state stale, causing (a) no frontend notification of MCP changes, and (b) inflated spurious deltas on the next regular command. The fix rebuilds GuiState via build_gui_state() after each MCP call, computes the delta against last_state, emits delta events, and wraps the call in emit_status('evaluating') + IdleGuard for evaluation-status consistency. The conservative approach of always syncing (even after read-only tools) is correct because build_gui_state is cheap for unchanged state and compute_delta produces an empty delta when nothing changed.

prerequisites.description: Add reify-mcp workspace dependency to gui/src-tauri/Cargo.toml and add mcp_context module declaration to lib.rs. Also add reify-mcp with test-support feature to dev-dependencies.

steps.description: Write failing tests for TauriToolContext read methods in tests/mcp_context_tests.rs. Create a helper make_tauri_context() that builds a TauriToolContext from a loaded EngineSession (reusing bracket_source() fixture). Test cases: (1) get_source returns SourceContent with bracket source text and file_path='bracket.ri'; (2) get_open_files returns vec with one OpenFileInfo (path='bracket.ri', language='reify', dirty=false); (3) get_parameters returns non-empty Vec<ParameterInfo> containing 'Bracket.width', 'Bracket.height', 'Bracket.thickness' with correct cell_id, name, value, unit, kind='Param', determinacy='determined'; (4) get_constraints returns non-empty Vec<ConstraintInfo> with status='Satisfied'; (5) get_eval_status returns EvalStatusInfo with phase='idle', dirty_count=0; (6) get_selection returns SelectionInfo with selected_entity=None, hovered_entity=None; (7) get_source_location('Bracket.width') returns SourceLocationInfo with file='bracket.ri', line>=1; (8) get_source_location for nonexistent entity returns EngineError; (9) get_diagnostics returns empty Vec.

Create gui/src-tauri/src/mcp_context.rs with TauriToolContext struct holding engine: Arc<Mutex<EngineSession>> and event_emitter: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&str, serde_json::Value) + Send + Sync>>. Implement ReifyToolContext read methods: get_source locks engine, reads source_map entry; get_open_files maps source_map to OpenFileInfo with dirty=false; get_parameters/get_constraints call engine.build_gui_state() and convert ValueData->ParameterInfo, ConstraintData->ConstraintInfo; get_eval_status returns idle; get_selection returns empty; get_source_location delegates to EngineSession::get_source_location; get_diagnostics returns empty vec. Add constructors: new(engine) and with_event_emitter(engine, emitter).

Write failing tests for TauriToolContext write methods in tests/mcp_context_tests.rs. Test cases: (1) update_source with valid new source returns UpdateResult{success:true}; (2) update_source with invalid source returns EngineError; (3) set_parameter('Bracket.width', '100mm') returns SetParamResult{success:true} with new_value='100' and unit='mm'; (4) set_parameter with invalid cell_id returns EngineError; (5) open_file reads .ri from disk and returns OpenFileInfo; (6) save_file writes source_map content to a temp file and returns Ok(true); (7) export('step', path) writes file to disk and returns Ok(true).

Implement TauriToolContext write methods: update_source locks engine, calls EngineSession::update_source, returns UpdateResult; set_parameter locks engine, calls EngineSession::set_parameter, then extracts the new formatted value/unit from the returned GuiState by finding the matching cell_id; open_file calls EngineSession::load_file; save_file writes source_map content to disk path; export parses format string and delegates to EngineSession::export. All errors are mapped to ToolError::EngineError.

Write failing tests for navigation/event methods in tests/mcp_context_tests.rs. Test cases: (1) focus_entity with event_emitter records ('focus-entity', entity_path) event and returns Ok(true); (2) navigate_to_source with event_emitter records ('navigate-to-source', {file, line, column}) event and returns Ok(true); (3) focus_entity without event_emitter returns Ok(true) (best-effort, no crash); (4) navigate_to_source without event_emitter returns Ok(true).

Implement focus_entity and navigate_to_source: if event_emitter is Some, call it with the event name and JSON payload. Always return Ok(true). Use serde_json::json! macro to construct navigate-to-source payload with file, line, column fields.

Write failing tests for mcp_tool_call_impl dispatch in tests/mcp_dispatch_tests.rs. Test cases: (1) dispatching 'reify_get_eval_status' with empty params returns JSON with phase='idle'; (2) dispatching 'reify_get_source' returns JSON with bracket source content; (3) dispatching 'reify_set_parameter' with {cell_id:'Bracket.width', value:'100mm'} returns JSON with success=true; (4) dispatching unknown tool 'nonexistent' returns error; (5) dispatching 'reify_get_parameters' returns JSON array with expected parameter entries.

Add mcp_tool_call_impl function to mcp_context.rs: takes (name: &str, params: serde_json::Value, context: &dyn ReifyToolContext) -> Result<serde_json::Value, String>. Creates a ToolRegistry, calls register_all_tools, then call_tool. Maps ToolError to String. Export this function publicly.

Write compile-time assertions in tests/mcp_context_tests.rs: (1) TauriToolContext implements Send + Sync (fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() called with TauriToolContext); (2) TauriToolContext can be wrapped in Arc<dyn ReifyToolContext> (construct and verify).

Add mcp_tool_call Tauri command to main.rs: #[tauri::command] fn mcp_tool_call(app: AppHandle, state: State<AppState>, name: String, params: Value) -> Result<Value, String>. Constructs TauriToolContext::with_event_emitter using a closure that calls app.emit(). Calls mcp_tool_call_impl. Add to generate_handler! list. No event emission for eval status needed since MCP calls are synchronous.

Write failing test `mcp_write_tool_produces_state_delta` in tests/mcp_dispatch_tests.rs that validates the state-sync pattern needed by the Tauri wrapper. The test: (1) creates an engine with bracket_source, builds initial GuiState via build_gui_state(), stores it in a Mutex<Option<GuiState>> (simulating AppState.last_state); (2) creates a TauriToolContext and calls mcp_tool_call_impl('reify_set_parameter', {cell_id:'Bracket.width', value:'100mm'}) — confirming the MCP write succeeds; (3) rebuilds GuiState from the engine via build_gui_state(); (4) calls compute_delta(&last_state, &new_gui_state); (5) asserts the delta's changed_values is non-empty (width value changed from 80 to 100); (6) asserts last_state was updated by compute_delta (no longer the initial snapshot). Also write a companion test `mcp_read_tool_produces_empty_delta` that performs the same flow but with a read-only tool ('reify_get_parameters'), asserting the delta's changed_values/changed_constraints/changed_meshes are all empty — proving the conservative always-sync approach is safe for reads.

Fix mcp_tool_call Tauri command in main.rs to sync state and emit events after MCP tool dispatch, matching the pattern used by set_parameter/update_source/open_file_engine. Changes: (1) clone app before moving it into the event_emitter closure (let app_for_emitter = app.clone()); (2) add emit_status(&app, 'evaluating') before the dispatch call; (3) add let _idle = IdleGuard(app.clone()) for RAII idle emission; (4) after mcp_tool_call_impl returns (regardless of result, conservatively), lock the engine, call build_gui_state(), call compute_delta(&state.last_state, &gui_state), and call emit_delta(&app, &delta). This ensures last_state is always current after MCP mutations, the frontend receives immediate delta events for MCP-triggered changes, and evaluation-status events bracket the call for UI spinner consistency. The state sync runs even for read-only tools since compute_delta produces an empty delta when nothing changed, avoiding the need to categorize tools.

design_decisions.decision: Create TauriToolContext as a separate struct rather than implementing ReifyToolContext directly on AppState
design_decisions.rationale: AppState has additional fields (last_state, watcher) that are irrelevant to MCP and would create tight coupling. A dedicated context struct is cleaner and follows the same pattern as MockToolContext. It also avoids needing to make AppState Send+Sync-compatible with the ReifyToolContext trait bounds.

design_decisions.decision: Use a callback/closure for event emission (focus_entity, navigate_to_source) rather than storing tauri::AppHandle directly
design_decisions.rationale: Keeps TauriToolContext testable without a Tauri runtime. Tests can provide a closure that records emitted events into an Arc<Mutex<Vec>>. The actual AppHandle-based emitter is only constructed in the mcp_tool_call Tauri command. This follows the same principle as the _impl function pattern used by existing commands.

design_decisions.decision: Return 'idle' for get_eval_status since evaluation is synchronous in the current architecture
design_decisions.rationale: EngineSession methods are blocking and return only when evaluation completes. The 'evaluating' phase is tracked by the Tauri command wrappers in main.rs via IdleGuard, not by the engine itself. MCP tool calls hold the engine lock while executing, so if the call returns, evaluation is done. Future async evaluation would need to track phase explicitly in AppState.

design_decisions.decision: Return empty vec for get_diagnostics initially
design_decisions.rationale: Diagnostics are pushed from the LSP bridge via TauriNotificationSink events and stored in the LspBridge, not in EngineSession. Wiring the LspBridge's stored diagnostics into the MCP context would require adding LspBridge access to TauriToolContext, which is a separate concern that can be added as a follow-up.

Selection state (hovered/selected entity) lives in the frontend 3D viewport (Three.js), not in the Rust backend. To implement this properly would require the frontend to push selection state back to the backend via a Tauri command. This is out of scope for the current wiring task.

design_decisions.decision: Build GuiState via EngineSession::build_gui_state() for get_parameters/get_constraints rather than accessing engine internals directly
design_decisions.rationale: EngineSession's build_gui_state() already handles the complex logic of iterating templates, formatting values with units, computing determinacy, collecting constraint expressions, etc. Using it avoids duplicating this logic. The conversion from ValueData->ParameterInfo and ConstraintData->ConstraintInfo is a simple field mapping.

design_decisions.decision: Construct ToolRegistry per mcp_tool_call invocation rather than caching in managed state
design_decisions.rationale: Registration is cheap (16 closures pushed to a Vec, no dynamic allocation beyond that). Caching would require managing registry lifetime in AppState or a separate managed type. If profiling shows overhead, a cached Arc<ToolRegistry> can be added later, but for an interactive CAD tool, MCP call frequency will be low (Claude invocations, not tight loops).

Unlike existing _impl functions which take &Mutex<EngineSession>, the MCP dispatch layer works with the abstract ReifyToolContext trait. This allows the same dispatch function to work with both TauriToolContext (production) and MockToolContext (testing), and matches the ToolRegistry::call_tool interface.

design_decisions.decision: Always sync state after MCP tool calls (conservative approach) rather than only after known write tools
design_decisions.rationale: Categorizing tools as read vs write would require maintaining a separate list or adding metadata to the registry, which is fragile and could drift. The conservative approach calls build_gui_state() + compute_delta() after every MCP call. For read-only tools, build_gui_state() is cheap (no re-evaluation), and compute_delta() produces an empty delta with zero events emitted. The negligible overhead is worth the simplicity and correctness guarantee.

reuse.what: ReifyToolContext trait, ToolRegistry, register_all_tools, ToolError, and all MCP response types
reuse.how: TauriToolContext implements ReifyToolContext; mcp_tool_call_impl uses register_all_tools() + ToolRegistry::call_tool() for dispatch; ToolError for error returns; ParameterInfo/ConstraintInfo/etc. as return types

reuse.what: EngineSession methods (set_parameter, update_source, load_file, export, get_source_location, build_gui_state)
reuse.how: TauriToolContext delegates all engine operations to EngineSession methods after acquiring the Mutex lock

reuse.what: Type conversion from ValueData/ConstraintData → MCP ParameterInfo/ConstraintInfo
reuse.how: Build GuiState via EngineSession, then map: ValueData.cell_id→ParameterInfo.cell_id, ValueData.name→ParameterInfo.name, etc. Fields have matching structure and semantics.
Reuse make_loaded_session() pattern: SimpleConstraintChecker + MockGeometryKernel + bracket_source() to create a loaded EngineSession for TauriToolContext tests
Follow same pattern: mcp_tool_call_impl as a free function taking concrete deps, thin Tauri command wrapper in main.rs

reuse.what: Existing Tauri command registration and event emission patterns
reuse.how: Add mcp_tool_call to generate_handler! list; use app.emit('focus-entity', ...) pattern for navigation events

reuse.what: compute_delta + emit_delta state-sync pattern from existing write commands
reuse.how: After mcp_tool_call_impl returns, call build_gui_state() then compute_delta(&state.last_state, &gui_state) to update last_state and get the diff, then emit_delta(&app, &delta) to notify the frontend. Reuse IdleGuard RAII pattern for evaluation-status events.
